Overall:
The following drinking water processes were found to be effective for the inactivation or removal of V. cholerae: chlorine, ultraviolet (UV) irradiation, and direct filtration (greater than 95 percent removal for V. cholerae attached to plankton). The performance of chlorine and UV technologies vary based on chemical dose, contact time and water quality characteristics.
As the bacterium is susceptible to heat, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention recommend one minute of vigorous boiling for inactivation. 2008
